evidence.finding: The retained Gross 2002 Figure 2 caption names methanol-isobutane and reports PC-SAFT k_ij = 0.05 and SAFT k_ij = 0.03 at 100 degC.
The retained Figure 2 source image labels the horizontal axis as x_Isobutane.
The figure manifest preserves the same methanol-isobutane caption and local source-image path.
The retained Table 2 extraction maps the same k_ij values to methanol-isobutanol.
The current analysis-local binary interaction source manifest maps the PC-SAFT k_ij = 0.05 row to methanol and 2-methyl-2-butanol, which conflicts with the Figure 2 caption and axis label.
The current analysis-local k_ij matrix contains k_ij = 0.05 for methanol with 2-methyl-2-butanol, so the model-generation parameter snapshot must be corrected before Figure 2 can be accepted.
Gross/Sadowski 2001 retains pure PC-SAFT parameters for isobutane: m = 2.2616, sigma = 3.7574, epsilon/k = 216.53, temperature range 113-407 K.
evidence.path: docs/papers/md/ePC-SAFT-Literature/Gross, Sadowski - 2001 - PC-SAFT An equation of state based on a perturbation theory for chain molec.md

rejected_systems.reason: Retained Table 2 text says isobutanol, but the Figure 2 caption and source-image axis identify isobutane.
The current analysis-local parameter snapshot maps the k_ij row here, but the Figure 2 caption and source-image axis identify isobutane.

resolution_reason: Accepted methanol/isobutane because both the retained Figure 2 caption and the plotted source-image axis identify isobutane. The retained Table 2 label methanol-isobutanol and current analysis-local parameter snapshot methanol/2-methyl-2-butanol are recorded as provenance conflicts that must be corrected before model generation or acceptance.
